schritte: Make the Sauce:

Add everything to a small pan. Cook on low-medium heat. Once the sauce starts simmering, cook for 3-5 minutes. Stir until the sauce is a darker red color, shiny and thickened. Turn off heat and set aside.
Prepare the Chicken:
Pat chicken dry with paper towel. Place the chicken in a bowl and add spices, and egg; mix until coated.
Then add flour, cornstarch, and baking powder; mix again. The chicken should be covered in a thick, sticky layer of batter. Add 1-2 tsp milk if batter is too thick.
Coat chicken with cornflakes:
Crush cornflakes into smaller flakes, then press the chicken thighs into them so they stick well.
Place the coated chicken on a wire rack for 5-10 minutes to set the coating.
Air fry or bake the chicken:
Spray chicken generously with oil. Air fry for 8 minutes, flip and continue cooking for another 6-8 minutes until deep golden, crispy and cooked through (165 F internal temperature).

To bake the chicken in the oven, preheat the oven to 425 F. Bake for 13 minutes, flip halfway and spray again with oil; continue baking for another 12-13 minutes until cooked through. For extra color, broil for 1-2 minutes.
Rest the chicken for 2 minutes.

schritte: Marinate beef for 15 minutes. Mix sauce ingredients in a bowl. Separate fresh noodles or prepare dried rice noodles according to instructions.
Heat pan over high heat. Sear beef in batches for 2–3 minutes until browned and slightly charred, letting it sit undisturbed for 1 minute before flipping. Remove from pan.

Reduce heat to medium high, then add oil, then onion, garlic and ginger. Stir-fry for 1–2 minutes until fragrant. Add Chinese broccoli stems and cook for 2 minutes, then add leaves and cook a further 1–2 minutes until wilted. Remove from pan.

Increase pan heat, add noodles to the hot pan and let sit undisturbed for 30 seconds to lightly char. Toss gently, being careful not to break the noodles, then add sauce and cook for 1 minute until evenly coated.

Return beef and vegetables to the pan and toss for 1–2 minutes until glossy and combined. Add bean sprouts and spring onions in the final 30 seconds. Serve immediately with chilli oil and chilli flakes if desired.

schritte: Marinate the chicken:
Blend together all the marinade ingredients. Coat the chicken with the marinade and leave it for atleast an hour or overnight.
Roast the vegetables:

Preheat the oven to 475 F. Place the poblano peppers, jalapeño, onions, tomatillos, and garlic on a sheet pan. Drizzle olive oil and salt, then bake for 15-20 minutes till blistered and softened. Optionally, broil for 2 minutes for extra char.
Prepare the Poblano Avocado sauce:
Transfer poblanos and jalapeño to covered bowl for 10 minutes. Then remove stems, peel most charred skin, and seeds if desired.

Transfer the roasted veggies along with the other ingredients (avocado ,crema, mayo, cilantro, lime juice, salt, honey, cold water) to a blender, and blend until silky and glossy. Chill until ready to use.
Cook the chicken:
Heat a cast iron or steel pan on high heat. Cook chicken until caramelised, and cooked through. Rest for 5 minutes, then slice.
Heat tortillas on hot pan 20-30 seconds per side until soft and blistered.
Assemble:
Layer the tacos with the roasted pepper avocado sauce, chicken, pico de gallo, pickled onions, cilantro and lemon juice. Enjoy!

schritte: Start by making the bravas sauce. Char the long red peppers, tomatoes, shallot and chilli until softened and slightly blackened. You can do this in a pizza oven, on a BBQ, under the grill, or in a hot dry pan.

Once nicely charred, add everything to a blender with the smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, olive oil, sherry vinegar, salt and pepper. Blend until smooth, then taste and adjust with more vinegar, salt or cayenne if needed. Set aside.

For the chicken, rub the spatchcocked chicken halves with olive oil, salt and black pepper. Cook in a hot pizza oven, on a BBQ, or under the grill until the skin starts to crisp and the chicken has good colour.
Once the chicken is nearly cooked, start brushing it with the bravas sauce. Keep cooking and brushing until the sauce becomes sticky, charred and slightly caramelised on the outside.
Cook the chicken until it reaches around 67°C in the thickest part, then rest for 10 minutes. Resting will bring it up to 70°C.
Serve the crispy bravas chicken with a generous spoonful of garlic aioli on the side, plus extra bravas sauce for dipping. #tasty #foodie #chicken #cooking

In a medium bowl, add mayonnaise, barbecue sauce, chipotle powder, and paprika.
Whisk the Mixture
Using a whisk, blend all the ingredients together until smooth and creamy. Ensure the spices are evenly mixed for consistent flavor.
Store or Serve
The sauce can be used immediately as a dip or spread. If you’re preparing ahead of time, store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator. Stir before serving for best results.
Optional: a pinch of Dijon mustard for extra depth
Start with the Garlic

For a bold flavor, use raw minced garlic. If you prefer something mellow, roast the cloves until soft and golden before mixing them in. Roasted garlic gives the aioli a sweeter, more subtle taste that pairs beautifully with roasted vegetables or seafood.
Emulsify the Base

In a mixing bowl, whisk the egg yolks with lemon juice until smooth. Slowly drizzle in the olive oil, a few drops at a time at first, whisking constantly. As the mixture thickens, you can add the oil a little faster. The goal is a smooth, creamy emulsion.
This step is where the magic happens. With patience, you’ll see the yolks and oil transform into a silky base that’s perfect for holding the garlic flavor.

Once the aioli base is thick, whisk in the minced or roasted garlic. Taste and adjust with a little more salt or lemon juice if needed. If you want a punchier aioli, add another clove. For something smoother, stick to one or two.

schritte: Warmes Wasser, warme Milch, Zucker und Hefe in einer Schüssel verrühren und ca. 5 Minuten stehen lassen. In der Zwischenzeit ein Ei verquirlen.
Mehl mit dem Salz vermischen. Butter zugeben und mit den Fingern einarbeiten, bis feine Krümel entstehen. Hefemischung und Ei unterrühren, bis ein grober Teig entsteht.
Den Teig etwa 10 Minuten kräftig kneten, bis er glatt, elastisch und leicht glänzend ist. Abgedeckt an einem warmen Ort ca. 1 Stunde gehen lassen.
Teig in 8 gleich große Portionen teilen, zu runden Buns formen und auf ein mit Backpapier belegtes Blech setzen. Weitere 60 Minuten gehen lassen.

Mit Milch bestreichen. Backofen auf 200 °C Ober-/Unterhitze vorheizen, eine ofenfeste Schale mit Wasser auf den Ofenboden stellen und die Buns ca. 15 Minuten goldbraun backen. Evtl. dabei nochmal bestreichen.
Alle Zutaten außer dem Öl fein mixen. Bei laufendem Mixer das Öl langsam einlaufen lassen, bis eine stabile Emulsion entsteht. Bei Bedarf einen Spritzer Wasser zugeben und abschmecken.
Entenkeulen vom Knochen lösen und grob zupfen. Mit den restlichen Zutaten marinieren und gut vermengen. Nach Geschmack nochmals abschmecken.

Wusstet ihr, dass ihr eine Entenbrust am besten in einer kalten…
schritte: Zuerst befreie ich die Entenbrüste mit einem scharfen Messer von den oberflächlichen Sehnen. Dafür gehe ich vorsichtig unter die Silberhaut und führe das Messer mit leichtem Druck daran entlang. Überstehendes Fett wird entfernt.
Die Hautseite schneide ich rautenförmig ein – dabei wirklich nur das Fett einschneiden, nicht das Fleisch. Anschließend salze ich die Entenbrüste von beiden Seiten.

Damit die Haut gleichmäßig bräunt, kann man die Entenbrüste leicht beschweren, sodass sie plan in der Pfanne liegen. Nach etwa 5 Minuten ist die Haut goldbraun und schön knusprig. Dann werden die Brüste gewendet.

Nun lege ich die Entenbrüste auf ein Gitter oder Blech und gebe sie bei 160 °C für etwa 8 Minuten in den Ofen. Danach lasse ich sie 5 Minuten ruhen. Erst jetzt werden sie aufgeschnitten und mit frisch gemahlenem Pfeffer gewürzt.
Je nach Größe der Entenbrüste muss die Garzeit angepasst werden. Wer es besonders zart mag, kann die Ofentemperatur auch etwas reduzieren und die Garzeit leicht verlängern.

schritte: Start by making the jam! Heat a heavy bottom skillet over medium high heat, then add the diced bacon. cook for 10-15 mins until crispy with a slight chew. Drain and remove from the pan.

Remove the grease from the pan, but leave the drippings and bits of bacon in there. Then add the diced white onion and saute for 3 mins.  Then add the Coffee, brown sugar, and raspberries and stir to combine. Once the berries start to break down, add the salt and pepper, then put the bacon back in the pan.
Simmer on medium low for 45-50 mins, or until the Bacon Jam has thickened up.  Remove and set aside while make the burgers.
Heat a skillet or griddle over medium high heat, then place down the 75/25 ground beef.  Then place some of the thinly sliced jalapeños on top before smashing and then season with the Jalapeno Salt.
Carefully flip the burgers after about 90 seconds, and then top them off with Havarti cheese. Let them cook for 30 more seconds while the cheese melts, then remove.

To build the burger, grab a pretzel bun and add whipped cream cheese to the bottom. Then place down two of the jalapeno Patties before covering the top bun in the bacon jam! Place the top bun down, and enjoy!

schritte: Start with the lamb. I used a mix of tender lamb neck and lamb leg steak - 300g is plenty. Get your butcher to mince these, or do it at home. Can also use pre-minced lamb
To a food processor, add 1-2 long green peppers, 1 sweet red pepper, 1 red chilli, 3 plum tomato’s, 3 cloves garlic - blitz together and strain out excess liquid

Add minced lamb to a bowl, then grate in 1 whole onion and add in spiced mixture + a handful chopped parsley, sea salt, black pepper, 2tsp pul biber, 1tsp spicy red pepper flakes, 1tsp red pepper paste, 2tbsp tomato paste and 1tbsp olive oil
Mix together till evenly incorporated - should have a spreadable consistency
For the dough: add 1tsp sugar to 180ml warm water, mix to dissolve, then add 1.5tsp instant yeast and mix - leave for 10 mins till foamy, then add to 300g AP flour and season with 1tsp salt

Mix and knead till you form a smooth dough, then add to a greased bowl and cover with damp cloth for 15 mins. Punch out the air and shape into 60-70g balls, tucking in the edges to seal - cover with damp cloth for 10 mins
Knead and stretch out dough till nice and thin, then spread on meat mixture in a thin layer. Don’t pile it on heavy, it should be light
I baked this in my @gozney dome - heated to 350c on the stone, then turned heat right down once placed inside. Swivel a few times till sizzling and evenly browned, then remove and pile them up!
Serve with a bunch of parsley in centre, along with some thinly sliced red onion seasoned with sumac, lemon juice and olive oil - squeeze lemon juice all over, then roll up all together and chow down

titel: The Carajillo
Launching something exciting very soon - head to the link in my bio…
schritte: Get yourself 225g high quality dates. Medjool are good but make sure you taste them first and are happy with the sweetness / flavour
Push out the pits with a chop stick, then chop up well and add to saucepan
Add 200ml water and simmer for 10 mins on med-heat to soften, then add 1tsp baking soda and mix thoroughly as it foams - then use an immersion blender to blitz smooth

To a food processor or bowl, add 100g soft unsalted butter (I used @allthingsbutter__ ), 50g brown sugar, 1tbsp each treacle and golden syrup and mix till combined, then crack in 2 eggs and combine, followed by 1x pod of vanilla beans and then the date puree
Mix till combined, then add 175g AP flour, 2tsp baking powder and mix one last time. It should be a light and fluffy mixture

Grab a baking tray and line with butter, then pour the mixture in and bake at 180c for ~25-40 mins - timing varies depending on tray size and depth, so use a chopstick to check doneness after 20 mins, by poking it to the bottom. It should come out fairly clean, not wet. Edges should be set and centre should have a very slight jiggle. If not done, bake a little more
Make the sticky toffee sauce: to a saucepan, add 150g unsalted butter, 250g dark muscovado sugar, 1tbsp treacle + golden and melt together for 4-5 mins on medium
Add in 250ml double cream and your vanilla pods from step 4 - mix to combine and let simmer and thicken for 5-10 mins - finish with sea salt
Remove the cake from the oven and poke a few holes all over, then pour over your hot sticky toffee sauce
Allow this to seep in for ~10-15 mins, then serve and top with vanilla bean ice cream, a drizzle of the sticky toffee sauce and a touch of sea salt
Words can’t describe these flavours. It’s so rich, so comforting and so indulgent. Intense flavour with great balance. Enjoy! #notoriousfoodie 🙅🏽‍♂️

titel: Home Cooking Sessions: Pork and Prawn Wontons with the most amazing…
schritte: Start with the filling. I used 250g pork mince and 150g king prawn. Add to a bowl along with: 3 cloves grated garlic, 1 knob grated ginger, 2tbsp finely minced leek, 2tbsp minced Chinese cabbage, 2 minced spring onions, 2tbsp chopped chives, 1tsp light soy, 2tbsp shaoxing wine, 1tbsp oyster sauce, 1tbsp sesame oil, 1tsp each white pepper, salt, sugar and MSG
Mix to combine thoroughly, then pick up and throw mixture into the bowl a few times to build up the texture
Add 1tsp of the filling to the centre of a wonton wrapper, then wet two edges and close to form a triangle. Wet the two corners and fold together, pressing to seal. Set aside in a cool place

Crispy chilli oil: finely slice one shallot and 10 cloves garlic 5. Add 500ml neutral oil to a pot and add in the shallots. Fry till golden and crisp, then remove. Add garlic to the same oil and repeat. Add blanched peanuts to the same oil and repeat - set all crunchy bits aside
In the same oil, add in 1 knob ginger, 3 cloves garlic, 2 star anise, 1 cinnamon stick, 1tbsp corriander seed, 2 bay leaves - leave to infuse on med heat ~15 mins then strain and remove aromatics
To a metal bowl, add 2tbsp gochugaru, 1tsp sichuan peppercorn powder, 1tsp chilli flake, 1tbsp sugar, 1tsp sea salt, 1tsp MSG - mix with 1tbsp black vinegar
Heat infused oil to 375f, then carefully add ladle by ladle to seasonings and stir. Keep adding till desired consistency, then add in all your crunch. Store in a jar and use for everything!!
Add your wontons to simmering water and cook for 4-5 mins
Add some chilli crisp to a bowl, along with black vinegar and your wontons straight from the water. Top with more chilli oil, black and white sesame seeds + spring onion
This one’s fire. The chilli crisp is unreal, I could literally put it on everything! Mouth-numbing flavour but with some sweetness to balance. Enjoy! #notoriousfoodie 🙅🏽‍♂️

titel: Mini-Apfel-Streusel-Törtchen
zutaten: 250 g Weizenmehl Typ 405 oder Dinkelmehl Typ 630
100 g Weizenmehl Typ 405 oder Dinkelmehl Typ 630
schritte: Alle Zutaten für den Mürbeteig rasch zusammenkneten. Der Teig kann ohne Kühlung direkt weiterverarbeitet werden.
Eine Muffinform mit Backtrennfett einfetten.

Den Mürbeteig in 12 Portionen teilen. Jede Portion rund formen und in jeweils eine Muffinvertiefung legen. Mit einem passenden Glas die Teigkugel platt drücken und dabei den Rand hochziehen. Das Glas zwischendurch in Mehl tauchen, damit es nicht klebt.

Für die Apfelfüllung 50 ml Apfelsaft abmessen und das Puddingpulver damit anrühren. Den restlichen Apfelsaft mit Zucker und Vanillezucker aufkochen. Das angerührte Puddingpulver einrühren und einmal aufkochen lassen, dann beiseitestellen. Die Äpfel mit Zitronensaft vermengen und unter den Apfelsaft-Pudding heben. Die Apfelfüllung in die Mürbeteig-Cups füllen.
Für die Streusel alle Zutaten krümelig kneten. Die Streusel auf der Apfelfüllung verteilen.
Die Apfeltörtchen im vorgeheizten Backofen bei 170 Grad Heißluft ca. 30-35 Minuten backen.
Die Törtchen in der Form abkühlen lassen. Vor dem Servieren mit Puderzucker bestäuben.

titel: 🍗 Coconut Lime Chicken with Rice 🥥🍋
schritte: Marinate the Chicken
In a bowl, combine coconut milk, lime juice, lime zest, minced garlic, soy sauce, ginger (if using), salt, and pepper.
Add the chicken to the mixture and let it marinate for at least 30 minutes (or overnight for extra flavor). 🥥🍋
Cook the Chicken
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and cook for 5-7 minutes per side, until golden brown and cooked through. Set aside. 🐔🔥
Prepare the Rice
While the chicken cooks, prepare the rice according to the package instructions. Fluff with a fork when done. 🍚
Make the Sauce
In the same skillet, pour the remaining marinade and bring it to a simmer.
Let it cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens slightly. 🌿
Assemble
Serve the chicken over the rice.
Drizzle with the coconut lime sauce and garnish with fresh cilantro and lime wedges. 🌟
Prep Time: 15 minutes
Marinate Time: 30 minutes (or overnight)
Cook Time: 20 minutes
Total Time: 1 hour 5 minutes
This Coconut Lime Chicken with Rice is a creamy and vibrant dish that will transport you straight to the tropics with every bite! Perfect for a satisfying, flavorful dinner! 😋

titel: THAI RED NOODLE SOUP 🍜 SOUP SEASON 2
schritte: Finely dice the carrot, onion + pepper.
Mince the garlic + ginger, bash the lemongrass stalk.
Add a splash of oil into a saucepan. On a medium high heat, add the carrot, onion + pepper with a pinch of salt. Sauté for 3-5 min until softened.
Add the garlic, ginger + lemongrass. Sauté for 1 min until fragrant.
Add the curry paste, mix well + allow to cook off for 2 min.
Add the coconut milk, then hit veg stock. Bring to a boil then reduce to simmer for 10-15 min. Season to taste with salt, lime juice + sweetener of choice if required.
For the toppings, in a hot pan sear + press out the moisture of the mushrooms. Add a splash of oil to coat + season with soy sauce.
For the tofu, press out some moisture + slice into chunks.
Coat well in cornflour + shallow fry in a glug of hot veg oil until golden + crisp on each side.  Season with soy sauce.
Cook the noodles according to indirections. Place into bowls, then top with ladles of broth. Add the mushroom, tofu + coriander on top. Garnish with chilli, spring onion + a wedge of lime - enjoy!

schritte: Start with the risotto!
Bring 1.5L chicken stock up to a gentle simmer, then add in some ground saffron and leave to infuse
Finely mince one shallot + 2 cloves garlic, then lace a pan with EV olive oil and bring to med heat
Add the shallots + garlic and sauté for 3-4 mins, then add 3-400g carnaroli rice and sauté for ~2 mins
Once combined and toasted, deglaze pan with a nice white wine ~250ml and reduce till almost gone, then ladle in your hot chicken stock a couple ladles at a time
Keep stirring till fully absorbed, then add in another two ladles - repeat until the rice is al dente and almost fully cooked
Finally, add in a generous amount of parmigiano reggiano ~65g and throw in 3-4tbsp unsalted butter
Meld vigorously to emulsify, using additional stock to loosen and create a flowy, creamy consistency
Pour out onto a baking dish lined with parchment and leave to cool, then place in fridge overnight
Remove from fridge and then use a ring mould to cut out a few discs

Bring a non-stick pan to med heat, add in a little butter, then place in your risotto disc to toast for 2 mins - heat management is vital here, too hot and it’ll become a mess, but too low and it won’t brown!
Use a plate to flip, then sear the other side

Fonduta: combine 150ml each whole milk + cream, bring to med heat, then throw in 100 cubed taleggio, 100g fontina, 50g parm, stir to melt. Once at 85c, add in 4 egg yolks and whisk to emulsify and thicken. Blitz till smooth and season to taste
Plate up with a good spoon of the fonduta, then place on your toasted risotto and top with flakey salt and chives

This was unreal. Inspired by my visit to @leos.london by chef @pep_belvedere - it’s an amazing dish and I am yet to perfect it. But if give this a go at home or head to his spot for the real deal! #notoriousfoodie 🙅🏽‍♂️

schritte: Get yourself a couple of 1-2lb lobsters
Dispatch each lobster, then throw into heavily salted boiling water and cook for 4-5 mins per pound
Once cooked, throw directly into an ice bath and leave them to cool - this stops the cooking process and prevents them from overcooking - super important!
Grab a bulb of garlic and slice off the end, then drizzle with olive oil, sea salt, black pepp and wrap with tin foil. Place into a 180c oven for 45 mins
Make the brown butter: add 500g unsalted butter to a cold pan and bring to med heat. Once melted, leave it to sizzle and crackle, it’ll start to foam slightly and turn golden - kill the heat here
To the brown butter, add in a little finely chopped celery + leaves, parsley, chives and dill

Remove garlic from the oven, squeeze out and mince with a knife, then add this to the butter, along with 1tbsp old bay, sea salt, celery salt, black pepp and juice of half a lemon - adjust seasoning to taste and set aside
Remove lobster from the ice bath and twist off the tails

Snap off the end of the tail, then press into itself and carefully work to release the tail meat. For the claws, use the back of a knife to crack open each side, then pull out the meat! See Saweetie’s technique for this - it works, just get some safety goggles 🥸
Chop lobster into evenly sized pieces, then add to your brown butter on med-heat to warm through
Meanwhile, slice off the sides of some brioche hot dog buns and toast in butter until golden
Once toasted, open the buns, add in a drizzle of kewpie, followed by a generous amount of lobster meat and drizzle over more of the butter
Top with a nice dollop of caviar and plenty of chives - i used @astrea_caviar
The brown butter is an essential part of this - adjust the seasoning to taste and enjoy! #notoriousfoodie 🙅🏽‍♂️

Coconut Curry Shrimp Recipe
zutaten: 1 lb (450g) large shrimp, peeled and deveined
1 cup (150g) snow peas or snap peas
1 cup (150g) baby spinach leaves or chopped bok choy
schritte: Season and Cook the Shrimp:
In a bowl, toss the shrimp with salt, pepper, and turmeric powder.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the shrimp and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side until just pink and cooked through. Remove and set aside.
Sauté Aromatics:
In the same skillet, add another tablespoon of oil if needed.
Sauté the chopped onion for 2-3 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger. Cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Prepare the Curry Sauce:
Add the curry paste to the skillet and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to release the flavors.
Pour in the coconut milk, chicken or vegetable broth, fish sauce, soy sauce, and brown sugar. Stir well to combine.
Simmer the Curry:
Bring the curry sauce to a simmer and let it cook for 5-7 minutes to thicken slightly.
Add the sliced red bell pepper and snow peas. Simmer for another 3-4 minutes until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Combine and Finish:
Return the cooked shrimp to the skillet, stirring gently to coat with the sauce.
Add the baby spinach or bok choy in the last minute of cooking and let it wilt into the curry.
Stir in the lime juice for a fresh, tangy finish.
Serve the coconut curry shrimp over jasmine rice or rice noodles.
Garnish with fresh cilantro or Thai basil and a squeeze of lime juice.

schritte: Throw your chicken thighs into a large bowl
Cover with buttermilk then add a glug of pickle juice + a dash of hot sauce, followed by: 1tsp garlic powder, 1tsp onion powder, 1tsp cayenne, 1tsp paprika, 1tsp Msg, sea salt, pepp
Mix, cover and refrigerate overnight. The brining is important. Do it
Hot sauce. Either use store bought or make your own. Takes a while but it’s very leng (10-day lacto-ferment) Roughly chop 7-10 hot chilli’s. I love scotch Bon!
Add to a mason jar with a 2 chopped sweet peppers + 4 cloves garlic, then cover in a 3% salt brine (~3g salt for every 100ml water)

Leave to ferment for 10 days, opening every day to release gasses. After 10 days, drain and add to a blender with a few spoons of the brine, 1 chopped mango, splash of apple cider vin, mirin, lime juice + lots of olive oil - blitz, strain then add to a saucepan
Cook this down on med heat. Simmer for 3-4 mins, then add splash of Worcestershire, 2tbsp honey, msg and lots of @allthingsbutter__
Stir to emulsify - taste, adjust any seasoning and finish with chives
Remove chicken from fridge 1h before cooking, then dredge: 50:50 rice flour to AP flour along with 1tsp each: garlic powder, onion powder, cayenne, paprika, sea salt, black pepp
Add a spoon of brine to the flour, mix, then add one chicken thigh at a time. Press and coat in the flour, shake off any excess
Throw the chicken into preheated neutral oil, set to ~350f and remove once golden and crisp ~4-5 mins, then leave to rest another 4 mins whilst you toast your brioche
Ranch: 4tbsp crème fraïche, 2tbsp kewpie mayo, splash of buttermilk, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, 1 clove grated garlic, dill, parsley, chives, sea salt, pepp, honey. Mix and adjust to taste
Place your chicken in a bowl, pour over the sauce and toss to coat
Assemble: brioche, ranch, sliced dill pickle, buffalo chicken, ranch!
The scotch bonnet and mango hot sauce is wild! No messing around. This one brings the heat #notoriousfoodie 🙅🏽‍♂️

schritte: Finely dice and then mince the lamb by hand. Hand-mincing is important as it helps maintain the texture; using a mincer can affect the consistency. Aim for a sticky mixture that still has some texture.
Finely dice 1 of the bullhorn peppers and squeeze out any excess moisture.
Season the lamb with sea salt, paprika, and the diced red pepper. Mix well to combine.

schritte: To make the dough, add the flour, 50g of lard, and warm water to a bowl. Mix until the dough has formed, then turn it out onto a floured surface and knead for 5-6 minutes or until the dough is smooth. Place the dough into a bowl, cover it, and let it rest for 1-2 hours or until it has doubled in size.
Once the dough has doubled, turn it out onto a lightly floured surface and roll it to roughly 3mm (1/8 inch) thick. Cut the dough into small squares using a pasta roller or a bench scraper.

Heat the oil in a pan to 160-170°C (320-340°F). Once the oil is hot, start frying the Gnocco Fritto, flipping them halfway through cooking. Fry in batches to avoid overcrowding the pan, which would cause the oil temperature to drop too quickly.
Once the Gnocco Fritto are fried, remove them from the oil and place them on a wire rack to drain.
Serve the Gnocco Fritto while they are still hot with sliced cured meats and cheese on the side. They make the perfect complement to any antipasto board.

Home Cooking Sessions: Moules-frites. A twist on the French classic…
schritte: Start with about 1kg of fresh mussels and add to some cold, heavily salted water. Leave the mussels to purge out any excess dirt / sand for 30 mins, then rinse thoroughly in fresh water till it runs clear and set aside
Peel 500g of Maris piper potatoes and then using a knife or mandolin, cut into thin slices. Again, rinse till water runs clear, then leave to soak in cold water in fridge for an hour
Finely dice up your mirepoix: 2 sticks celery, same amount fennel, leek, 1 shallot, 3 cloves garlic, 2 red chilli’s and ~100g chorizo
Add some ev olive oil to a pan, then throw in your chorizo and cook till golden and crispy on med-high ~7 mins
Throw in the rest of your mirepoix and sauté 3-4 mins until fragrant. Season, then add in some fresh herbs, I like thyme and bay
Throw in your mussels and sauté for 2 mins before deglazing with a nice white wine ~200ml, then cover with a lid and leave to simmer ~3 mins
In a separate pan, bring some neutral oil up to 350f - don’t fill pan more than half
Remove lid and discard any mussels that haven’t opened, then add 3tbsp crème fraïche + fresh parsley and shake pan to emulsify - kill the heat around here
Thoroughly dry your frites, carefully add to the oil and fry till golden and crisp ~5-7 mins
Once golden, remove, drain and season with rosemary salt, parmigiano and chives
Plate up with a bowl full of mussels, plenty of the braising liquid and a heaped pile of crispy frites

I could eat this a lot! Feel free to play with the flavours for the mussels. For the perfect fries, prepare ahead of time and once soaked, pat dry and leave in fridge to fully dry ahead of use! Enjoy #notoriousfoodie 🙅🏽‍♂️

titel: Jalapeño Shrimp in Garlic Butter Sauce
schritte: 16 oz shrimp uncooked , cleaned, deveined, tail off
1/2 teaspoon salt , black pepper, garlic powder, cayenne
1 teaspoon paprika
2 teaspoon olive oil
2 tablespoons chopped cilantro
1 teaspoon chili flakes - less if you don't want spicy
Garlic Butter Sauce
2 tablespoons butter
3-4 garlic cloves about 2 tablespoons garlic minced
1 jalapeño sliced - less if you don't want spicy
1 tablespoon honey
Juice of one lime
Pat dry your shrimp .
Using a small knife , cut a slit down the back of the shrimp to butterfly the shrimp .
Season with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, paprika , cayenne, olive oil and mix gently .
Chop your garlic and cilantro. Slice your jalapeño.

In a small pan make the butter sauce on very low heat by adding butter ( don't burn the butter ) . Then add garlic and mix and let garlic cook on low heat about 20 seconds. Add on lime juice and honey . Mix another 15 seconds and remove from the heat. Set aside .
In a skillet add one tablespoon olive oil and cook the shrimp. Cook one minute and then flip it over. Cook another one minute.
Drizzle the butter sauce and gently mix.
Add the jalapeño slices . Sprinkle chili flakes and cilantro. Give a mix and remove from heat.
Total cook time should not be more than 3-4 minutes as you don't want to dry and overcook the shrimp.

titel: Home Cooking Sessions: Carbonara. Revisiting the Roman classic. Just…
schritte: Start by dicing 200g of guanciale in to small pieces or cubes. If you can’t find this, just use pancetta.
Throw into a cold pan and bring to med-high. Allow the guanciale fat to render and for everything to start sizzling. Remove once golden and crisp ~7-10 mins
Strain some of this fat and set aside
Grate up 40g each of parmigiano reggiano and pecorino Romano - use the finest hole setting on the grater
Toast 5g of black peppercorns for 2-3 mins on med-high heat, then crush with a pestle and mortar
Crack 4-5 eggs and separate the yolks
Add the grated cheese, egg yolks, black pepper and a good splash of the reserved fat to a bowl and mix till smooth - you can reduce this amount to be honest but I like it saucy
Throw your pasta into boiling water and cook till al dente, then scoop out and reserve a jug of the pasta water
Reduce the heat to medium and throw a towel over the pan with pasta water, then place your bowl with the cheese mixture on top

Add in a ladle of the pasta water and mix to heat, emulsify and pasteurise the eggs. The key here is indirect heat so the eggs don’t scramble - the mix should be warm to the touch before adding the pasta and fairly loose in consistency
Once it’s smooth and glossy, add in your pasta along with more pasta water as required to coat and fully emulsify into a thick, creamy and cohesive sauce
Add in your crispy guanciale and toss together
Plate up and top with more black pepp, guanciale and any extra parm / pecorino or sauce
You just can’t go wrong with a carbonara. Dial up or down the black pepper to taste and enjoy. Remember to go easy on the heat when making that sauce! #notoriousfoodie 🙅🏽‍♂️

titel: Cajun SHRIMP in Garlicky Lemon Butter Sauce
schritte: One pound (16 ounces shrimp ) I used jumbo shrimp ) peeled, deveined - tail removed
1 tablespoon cajun seasoning
2 teaspoons paprika
2-3 tablespoons olive oil
10 ounces cooked pasta - spaghetti , angel hair or fettuccine
Garlicky lemon sauce:
One shallot or small onion diced
10 garlic minced - I used a lot
8 oz white wine or broth
1 teaspoon salt and white black pepper
1.5 teaspoon chili flakes - use less if you don't like spicy
Zest of one lemon
Juice of 1/2 lemon - add more if you like
1/4 cup chopped Italian flat parsley
4 tablespoons butter
Using a napkin dry off the shrimp.
Season with Cajun seasoning and paprika. Cajun seasoning is salty so NO need for salt .
Mince garlic and dice onion and parsley.
Cook pasta in salt water . Cook al dente and cook shy of 1-2 minutes of the box cook time. It will cook longer in the sauce.
Save 1/2 cup of starchy pasta water. Set aside.
Heat your pan . Get it hot enough to make water drops dance. THEN add olive oil. Add shrimp and cook only one minute each side and remove.
In same pan add shallot and onion and sauté 1-2 minutes on low heat. (Add olive oil if too dry ) .
Add chili flakes, lemon zest , lemon juice and deglaze with wine slowly.
Add salt , white or black pepper. Cook for a minute.
Add in the pasta, cooked shrimp and parsley. Mix gently.
Add pasta water if needed.
Garnish with more parsley.

titel: Szechuan Beef 🥩
zutaten: 2 red capsicums, sliced thin (red bell peppers)
thumb-sized piece of ginger, peeled and julienned
4 cloves of garlic, crushed and finely diced
2 large spring onions, sliced thinly, whites and green parts kept separate
schritte: Place the sliced beef in a bowl with 2 tablespoons of light soy sauce, 2 tablespoons of Shaoxing cooking wine, and 2 tablespoons of cornflour. Mix well and marinate.
Prepare all your vegetables so they are ready to cook.
Toast the Szechuan peppercorns in a dry pan for 1-2 minutes, or until they become fragrant. Transfer them to a mortar and pestle and pound them slightly, just to break them up a bit.
Make the sauce by combining chilli crisp, fermented bean paste, 1 tablespoon of light soy sauce, black vinegar, and 1 tablespoon of sugar in a bowl. Stir well.
Place a wok over high heat and add the peanut oil. Once the oil is hot, add the marinated beef and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes, or until the beef is browned. Remove the beef from the wok and set aside.
Place the walk back on the heat and add the red capsicums and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes. Then, add the ginger, garlic, and the white part of the spring onions. Stir-fry for a couple of minutes.
Add the toasted Szechuan peppercorns and the beef back into the wok. Stir-fry for 1-2 minutes before adding the sauce. Toss well to combine.
Serve the Szechuan beef garnished with the green part of the spring onions, alongside rice.

Home Cooking Sessions: Sweet and Sour Chicken. The Chinese classic…
schritte: Dice 2x chicken breasts or 4x thighs into bite-sized pieces and add to a bowl

Crack in 1 large egg and add 2tbsp light soy, sea salt, white pepper, 1tsp onion powder, 1tsp garlic powder, 1tsp MSG and mix to combine, then add in 2tbsp AP flour + 1tbsp corn starch, mix to combine - cover and leave in fridge for at least 1h
Dice some red, green and yellow peppers into bite sized pieces + half a red onion and finely mince 2 cloves garlic and 1 red chilli
For the sauce, combine 100ml chicken stock, 75ml ketchup, 50ml pineapple juice, 2tbsp caster sugar, 2tbsp white wine vinegar, 1tsp MSG, 1tbsp cornstarch - mix till smooth and set aside
Dry batter: equal parts AP flour + cornstarch, sea salt, white pepper, mix to combine
Add in a few pieces of your chicken at a time to the dry batter. Coat and firmly press in the flour to each piece so that they are all sufficiently coated and craggly. Repeat and set aside
Heat neutral oil to 350f and carefully lower in chicken a few pieces at a time - remove after 3-4 mins or till lightly golden and repeat till all are done
Allow oil to reach 375f, then fry again - place chicken back into oil and cook for further 1-2 mins till golden and crisp, then leave to drain on wire rack
Bring a wok to med-high, then add in some of the cooking oil and throw in all your vegetables. Sauté 2-3 mins, then add in your garlic + chilli and sauté 1 min
Pour in sauce and leave to simmer / reduce slightly, it should thicken after 2-3 minutes but let it go for ~5-7 mins, then add in your diced pineapple
Taste for additional seasoning, then add in chicken and toss to fully coat in the sauce
Plate up immediately with egg fried or plain jasmine rice
One of my favourite takeaway classics. There are a few methods and i love using a wet batter too, but find this less messy and crispier! Enjoy #notoriousfoodie 🙅🏽‍♂️

schritte: Get yourself 1kg of beef or lamb - I used short rib, chuck and oxtail - season with sea salt and leave to brine in fridge overnight
Pay dry, then sear in hot pan laced with neutral oil for 2-3 mins per side in batches, set aside
Throw in 1 diced white onion + 4 cloves sliced garlic, season & sauté ~5 mins then add in 4tbsp tomato paste and cook ~3 mins
Add in 3 diced tomatoes, then add back your beef, cover with beef stock and bring to a simmer

Toast your spices on med heat 2 mins then add them all to the broth: 3x guajillo chillies, 3x arbol, 2x ancho, 3 bay leaves, 1tbsp coriander seed, 1tbsp black peppercorn, 1x cinnamon stick, 1tsp cumin seed, sprig of thyme. I also add 1tbsp tinned chipotle chillies
Cover with lid and leave to simmer 1h, then remove chillies and blitz with 1 ladle of the broth. Add this back in to broth and leave to simmer additional 3h
Remove the beef and add to bowl with 2 ladels broth, cover with foil, let rest 1h - this is how you keep it juicy
Shred the meat and add 4-5 tbsp broth, then set aside
Allow broth to reduce further and season to taste
Toast your corn tacos on med heat for 1-2 mins, then dip into the broth before laying onto a med-high skillet laced with beef fat
Add a handful of shredded oaxaca cheese, allow to melt 1 min, then add 2 heaped tbsp shredded beef, 1tbsp broth and carefully fold over - allow to cook 1 min more then remove and repeat
Serve up with a bowl of your broth topped with diced white onion, coriander and a squeeze of lime - I paired with some @claseazulofficial Mezcal Guerrero!

titel: Home Cooking Sessions: Crème Brûlée. The creamiest, crunchiest,…
schritte: Start with some free range eggs. You’ll need about 8-10 yolks for a sharing style crème brûlée like this. Mine turned out a little yellow as these yolks are so orange!
Crack the eggs and add yolks to a bowl with 140g caster sugar - whisk until combined and set aside
In a saucepan, add in 850ml double cream (whipping cream) + 200ml whole milk + 2 scraped out vanilla beans and their pods

Bring to a gentle simmer to infuse the vanilla into the cream and stir for ~5-7 mins, then cover and set aside. For best results, chill the cream overnight and strain the next day for maximum vanilla infusion - alternatively, leave to cool for ~30 mins
Strain the cream then add it to your egg yolk mixture and fold together to emulsify into one cohesive mixture
Pour the mixture into a baking dish and use a blowtorch to quickly pop any surface bubbles
Add to a baking tray and fill up about half way with boiling water (this stops a skin forming on the top)
Place into a preheated oven set to 140c - the cook time really depends on the depth of your dish. Mine is about 4.5cm deep and I cooked for about 37 mins
Carefully remove and allow to come to room temp - then cover and place in the fridge overnight
Remove from fridge and top with granulated brown sugar or golden caster sugar (I used the latter) - pour enough for a nice even layer, then start to blowtorch and caramelise
Top with your choice of berries and fresh mint, then crack in and enjoy!

titel: Chili Garlic Salmon
schritte: Mix the soy sauce, olive oil, garlic, red pepper flakes, sesame oil, and honey in a bowl. Set aside 1/2 C of the sauce for serving.
+ STEP 2: PREP SALMON
Slice the salmon into large chunks. Add the salmon to a bowl.
+ STEP 3: MARINATE
Pour 1/2 cup of the sauce over the salmon and mix well to fully coat. Refrigerate for 2-4 hours, or at least 30 minutes.
+ STEP  4: AIR FRY
Transfer the salmon to the Air Fryer and cook at 400°F for 5-7 minutes.
+ STEP 5: SRIRACHA MAYO
Combine the mayo and Sriracha and set aside.
Top with the remaining sauce, Sriracha mayo, sesame seeds, and sliced chives.
You’ll find this recipe and other delicious recipes on the website. Cheers!

Home Cooking Sessions: Red Prawn Linguine with Carabinero Tartare.…
schritte: Get yourself 6-8 red carabinero prawns. These were from my good friends at @mr_txuleta - the very best seasonal produce!
Twist off the heads, tails and carefully peel off the shells, then devein, rinse and set aside

Slice and mince your prawns into a tartare, I do 3 finely chopped and 3 coarsely chopped - season with 2tbsp ev olive oil (this @tenuta_sanguido olive oil is amazing), @maldonsalt sea salt, zest of 1 lime and juice of 1/4 lemon - adjust to taste and keep refrigerated

Lace a med-high pan with olive oil and throw the prawn shells + heads. Season with sea salt + black pepp and sauté for 3 mins, then add in mirepoix: 1 each diced carrot, celery and shallot + 3 cloves sliced garlic + 1 red chilli - sauté for further 3 mins
Add in 2tbsp tomato paste, then deglaze with 300ml white wine and sauté for 4-5 mins
Reduce heat to gentle simmer add 500ml of cold water + 4/5 ice cubes to cool down the stock & maximise extraction of flavour
Once bubbling again, add in your herbs: 3 bay leaves, parsley, thyme, chives, basil - leave to simmer for 30 mins on low, then remove herbs
Remove from heat, then blend the mixture with the shells and strain out your beautiful prawn bisque
Add bisque to a large pan, bring to a boil, then add in your linguine to cook in the sauce - keep moving so it doesn’t stick together and gradually pour in more bisque till pasta is al dente
Once al dente, add 2-3 knobs of butter and emulsify, using the bisque to loosen as required
Twirl up the linguine, place into a bowl, spoon over the pan sauce, followed by your prawn tartare, a dollop of Oscietra caviar and chives
An intensely flavourful prawn linguine. The sauce has so much depth and the prawn tartare is naturally sweet and fresh with the citrus. Love cooking pasta this way! #notoriousfoodie 🙅🏽‍♂️
